page: Microsoft Azure D64as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dav6 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with multithreading enabled at 2.0 threads per core, running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The CPU cache configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache.

This server does not include local storage, but it is Premium Storage capable, allowing users to attach external high-performance storage volumes. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX, AVX-512 (specifically avx512f, avx512_vnni, and avx512_bf16), and cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. Virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the D64as_v6 demonstrates strong multi-core performance, achieving a Geekbench multi-core score of 22,176.0, which places it in the top 10% tier. Stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Passmark CPU scores also rank in the top 25%. Memory performance is characterized by a high read-only small-block cached bandwidth of 4,862.58 GB/sec and a write large-block bandwidth of 108.16 GB/sec, though uncached large-block read bandwidth is average at 151.2 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations are highly efficient, with Redis reaching 86.52 million operations per second.

Given its balanced vCPU-to-memory ratio and lack of local storage, this instance is well-suited for enterprise-grade general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and database operations. It also supports small to medium-sized language model inference, delivering strong text generation speeds of 31.6 tokens/sec on 7B models, though performance drops to an average tier of 3.45 tokens/sec for larger 70B models.
description: Microsoft Azure D64as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dav6 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz. It features 256.0 GB of RAM, translating to a balanced 4.0 GB per core. While it lacks local storage, it supports Premium Storage for external disk attachments. The instance excels in multi-threaded performance, ranking in the top 10% for Geekbench multi-core benchmarks and showing strong results in database operations, Redis SET performance, and software compilation. It supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instructions, making it highly capable for secure, compute-intensive tasks. This server is optimized for general-purpose enterprise applications, web serving, database hosting, and moderate LLM inference workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance for databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64as_v6 is a 64-vCPU general-purpose VM with 256 GB RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor for enterprise workloads.
tagline: A balanced 64-vCPU virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and 256 GB RAM for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores.
Equipped with 256 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.
Lacks local storage but supports high-performance Premium Storage attachments.
Top-tier multi-core performance in Geekbench and strong database operation benchmarks.
Supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instruction sets for accelerated computing.
Best suited for enterprise applications, database hosting, web serving, and software compilation.
